Hello everyone. I believe we've started this month's installment of our Goal Plus Call. This is Bill Glazer and I have with me today Dan Kennedy as always and Robert Ringer who is our guest expert. Let me tell everybody a little bit about Robert. He's the author of seven books including three number one bestsellers, Restoring the American Dream, Looking Out for Number One, and To Be or Not to Be Intimidated which is the updated and totally rewritten version of Winning Through Intimidation. He has appeared on numerous national talk shows including the Tonight Show. Today, Good Morning America, the Montel Williams Show, and the ABC News Nightline. In addition he has been the subject of featured articles in such major publications as Time, People, The Wall Street Journal, Fortune, Barons, and The New York Times and not to mention perhaps his biggest achievement which is being featured on today's Goal Plus Call. So Robert, Dan, Kenny, and I are honored to have you with us today. How are you? Perfect and I appreciate the invitation. Looking forward to the discussion. Well great. Let's just get started right into it. What would you say is the biggest message that your new book delivers? Well the message is conveyed in the title. The message is that you can have all the knowledge and wisdom in the world and you can have the greatest ideas in the world and you can do all the preparation that you can ever imagine. And today most people, not I shouldn't say most, but a lot of people have become preparation freaks but no matter how much knowledge and wisdom you have and no matter how great your idea is, no matter how much preparation you do, they're all completely useless without action because action is the starting point of all progress. If you look action up in the dictionary which I did for the first time a few years ago, it's a very straightforward forward definition. Action is the causation of change. So it's action that takes that idea which is nothing more than a theoretical and every person out there I'm sure is sitting with one or two great ideas but it's a theoretical until action causes a change and lifts it off a perception reality and converts it into something concrete. I'm a huge fan of the earlier work. In fact, winning through intimidation, God knows how many people I've convinced to read that book. I rank it as one of the 10 most influential in my library and a whole concept that I teach called take away selling is directly derived from my experiences applying what I learned from your book. So I'm a huge fan of the earlier work and I'm curious actually of how you moved from or what brought you from focus on to be intimidated or not to the new topic of this requirement to take action. Well, that's an interesting thing. I had a, in fact my keynote address is, address it now are always on this whole subject because sometimes the seemingly most simple things that are right before us all the time, we don't really think about them and Napoleon Hill said a great thing years ago in thinking Gros Rich, he said when you're ready for a thing it will make its appearance and we've all been somewhere where we hear somebody say something and we get all excited and maybe somebody were with a spouse or a business associate says, hey, what do you get so excited about? You've heard that so many times before and what happens is it's just the right person says it in just the right way at the right time and you get very excited and you're ready for the thing. So it gives the illusion, it's just a play on words. The truth is it's been there all the time but it just gives the illusion of just making its appearance. Now I'm not here to promote other books and this actually was somewhat of a coincidence. I didn't do this because I was going to be on this interview but I've just read a little book that some of you may have heard of. It's got a somewhat long title. It's called No BS Time Management for Entrepreneurs the Ultimate No Holds Barred Kick But Take No Prisoners Guide to Time Productivity and Sanity. And I got to tell you that there were endless things in that book that you said that I knew but you said them in just the right way in the right time and I just finished it about three, four days ago and I got to tell you that so many things you said in that book had a great great impact on me and that's the way I felt about action. I had heard this quote, what started it all is I'd heard this quote by Albert Einstein and Einstein said nothing happens till something moves and when Einstein used to talk he'd have this little twinkle in his eye where he was kind of sending you a message that what he's saying was true scientifically but it was also true in your everyday life and it was just the right time, the right place. It just registered with me. I've always been an action person but I said wow that's really profound. I like to call it simplistically profound and I thought to myself that is so true and I started intellectualizing this whole subject of action. Now when people here intellectualize they sometimes think oh wow I don't want to hear anything intellectual because that's really boring and everything. Well all intellectualized means is to give something a rational structure and so I really started thinking about this whole subject of action. I begin when I intellectualize something I begin by looking it up in the dictionaries. I said the dictionary says action is the causation of change and the more I looked into it the more I started to put together what in the book or I think I refer to them in the first edition as pillars in the second edition I refer to them as insights. These four insights into action that I would say have had a more dramatic impact on my life over the last few years than everything else that I've learned in the previous 25 to 30 years. I mean it just it sounds crazy because you think Robert Ringer is an action person. I mean I published my own books. I had a book that was turned down by 23 publishers and I didn't know anything about the book publishing business. I published it myself. It became a New York Times bestseller. As you know one of the one of my two books that are ranked by the New York Times is two of the 15 bestselling motivational books of all time. So I've always been an action person but I was sort of doing it by the seat of my pants. I was sort of doing it maybe it was just genetically for lack of a better word but when I started intellectualizing it I started really realizing how powerful it really is. You said something in your book Dan that really struck home with many things struck home with me but one that really struck home with me is how that you have to you said something about you know that there are guys and forgive me because I'm paraphrasing. You know there are a lot of guys who looked at you when you were on the Peter Lowe circuit and who were either thinking or saying right out to you well I mean I'm as good as you I'm better than you and you were humble enough to say and you know some of them were probably right but you never really got specific but I knew exactly what you were talking about. What I read into it is you were saying but I did something about it kind of like that little anecdote that I tell in the book about Paul Simon everybody said well anybody could write a music based on the sounds of Africa you didn't have to do anything and he looked at the interviewer and he said that's true anybody could do it but I did it. Well I yeah I'll move on to another topic which is actually this relates back Dan to something that you teach a Renegade millionaire that Robert pointed out in his book and he talked about the fact of the people that you spent time with the people that you hang around with can oftentimes I think you call it people taxes Robert in your book and that you know and you know Dan you know you in Renegade millionaire you spend quite a bit of time talking about you know Renegade millionaires they are very deliberate about the choosing the people that they spend time with and so Robert what is your recommendation for dealing with what you call people taxes maybe you ought to for the folks didn't get to that yet maybe you could identify what you mean by that. Well I have one of my weaknesses is that I have problems dealing with people who are incompetent even stupid and above all negligent or have a nasty attitude and I list those all in the people taxes section it's a definite weakness it's a deficit because and the result is I either end up cutting them off or I end up doing it myself which has been a big problem for me and I've fought it for years and years and years but people who and I go into great detail on the different kinds of people we don't have time to go in into them here but people who are some of the ones that I list people who get their nose out of joint easily I mean I get people I'll say something funny in my e-letter or in a book and I'll get an email or a letter from somebody who's just mad as anything and you want to tell them but you don't want to take the time I think you missed the joke you know I was just being humorous there you got to keep people like that out of your life of course people who are just downright dishonest how they're going to drain you at time they're going to drain you money they're going to train drain you of energy there's an endless list I mentioned the disjointed knows that the people who are nasty I won't deal with people who are nasty you know you show me that side of you you're gone and it's it's very difficult sometimes because and of course maybe the most important of all is people who are negative you cannot afford to have negative people in your life because this isn't just you know zig-zagular positive mental attitude talk the fact is that and I'm sure it's scientific if you got the bottom of it if you're not enough negative thoughts you know you are going to start thinking negative things it's just it's just uh it's um it's just to me it's a law of nature I mean it's just a natural phenomenon that uh that you can't avoid so you're going to be taxed of your time your energy in some cases money if you don't if you don't have the self-discipline and that's the key that's that's my strong suit is self-discipline I I can I can I always feel like I can outlast and outwork anybody when it gets to the to the crunch time and you got to have the self-discipline to be able to cut these kind of people out of your life I said no matter what else you do wrong if you if you take enough action you're going to succeed uh I'm not sure that maybe the other side of that coin is no matter what else you do right if you have enough of these kinds of people in your life negative people people who are nasty people who are rude and so forth you probably anything you do right you're still going to fail because you only have and Dan is the master at this which I found out from his book you only have so many hours in a day and I think in the book I break down the number of hours you have if you're like 30 years old you can live to be 80 that means that you've got a total of about 50 years or 18,250 days 2600 weeks or 600 months to do something instructive with your life so if you're 80 years old you're going to be 30 and excuse me 30 years old you're going to live to be 80 you have 600 hours left to do something instructive with your life but that's the good news the bad news is that you spend about a 30 year life sleeping and another 30 year life working what I call the daily cares of life things like getting up in the morning getting ready for work getting you know getting dressed getting ready for work uh getting ready for bed at night taking care of personal hygiene needs going to the doctor crummy stuff you have to clean up on weekends that you don't enjoy and it's not constructive so right off the bat you got to knock two-thirds off and instead of 50 years if you're only 30 years old you're going to live to be 80 you only have about 17 years left to do something instructive with your life instead of 18,250 days you only have about 6,000 days instead of 2600 weeks you got eight or 900 weeks and instead of 600 months you got 200 months to do something instructive with your life now how in the world are you ever going to do anything constructive let alone great if you're spending an inordinate amount of time dealing with people who are rude nasty negative lazy negligent incompetent it's not a matter of being a being a bad person you just don't you can't afford it you're not that rich in time you can't afford it do you have i teach three do you have a like a litmus test that you use one or more to determine um early as quickly and ruthlessly as possible who you want to be in a deal with or associate with or do business with and who you don't yeah uh yes i have an absolute litmus test the first test and uh and i again i think dan direct me if i'm wrong you may have said this somewhere but i've i felt this way for over two decades the test number one is only deal with people who want to deal with you that is absolutely essential in my first book which you said you you read you may recall something called the um the makeable deal theory absolutely and that's the same principle and what i learned when i was a young real estate broker and boy i i was really uh i thought i was a real big shot but i came from somebody who people just ignored cheated uh you know the story about how they grab my chips uh every deal every real estate deal was one commission apart so i was the unwanted party get lost and what i did is over a period of time i threw the kind of hard work that dan is talking about in his productivity book his time book is i just kept looking and trying and flying here and flying there making calls it was activity and i was able to start finding people my big breakthrough was uh a real estate investment trust who had guys at the head of it who were honest who thought boy this is really a hot shot young entrepreneur they liked me and i found that one makeable deal and for the time i found them uh i made almost a million dollars which is over two million dollars in today's in today's world uh in real estate commissions the first time i found them uh first year that i did that and i implemented my perception preceding reality strategy which was really my leapfrog theory which was really based on my earth brochure and i'm sure my and most of your callers don't even know what i'm talking about but you would dan if you read i copied it okay well hopefully you did well with it uh because it's always it's always worked for me and uh that's the first thing so that's the first thing you have to do instead of working on a hundred deals that are unmakeable or or extraordinary long shots spend that time working to find that one deal that works now dan mentions in his book that he does one thing a day to try to find new business and it doesn't sound like very much but that's 21 or two days and in a month you know and in over a period of a year that's 250 times and as you know from reading the book the law of averages which everybody thinks they understand and they do intellectually but that was one of my big things that i tied into action it just made absolute sense that uh the law of averages everybody knows about it but they don't really put it to their advantage we learn at a young age the more action we take the more results we get but it doesn't seem to really kick in with people because they they don't really take advantage of it and then that's when you go on to the next step and learn time is your best friend because i will look anybody in the eye and say you give me enough time i'm going to beat you i'm going to win this game because i know a secret the more action i take the more results i'm going to get it maybe one out of three one out of five one out of ten one out of a hundred but i know the law of averages works therefore all i need is time don't give me too much time if you want to beat me if you give me enough time i'm going to win the game because i'm relentless and uh i don't know where this question started out but i hope i hope that point gets across to your listeners because it's an important point you can't lose with the law of averages if you're if you have enough time while we were talking about makeable deals don't spend your time on deals that are long shots and the longest shot in the world and i learned this in the real estate business is a person who either doesn't want to do business with you sees you as just an an annoyance in the deal which is the way they used to see most of them used to see real estate brokers or a person who doesn't want the business bad enough nothing turns me off then a person who makes it it lets it be known that they don't need the business they don't need you they don't need anything they're perfectly happy with they got i'm not going to try to sell a guy like that because that's an uphill sale now in my case i have a little bit of a high profile so i've always said and there's nothing i don't mean anything egotistical by because everyone on the phone has something going for them like this in this case it's my name so i know that i'm on the i'm on google.com 330 000 hits or whatever and three number one best sellers and i feel this way this is there's no egotism in this at all it's just a pragmatic thing why should i deal with the person who says you know i you had to excuse me but i really don't know who you are and it happens a lot especially because i disappeared for so long that happens to me a lot uh now i've never heard any of your books i don't particularly want to deal with someone like that because i think to myself it's too easy for me to pick up the phone or go on to the next guy who says oh my gosh you're robert ringer you've got to be kidding are you the robert ringer i'm going to say it again it's not egotism but can you see uh bill dan where i've already got my foot three quarters away in the door if the guy's already read my books and loves me why should i why should i try to sell somebody on myself if i've already got that advantage and even if your listeners are thinking well but i'm not a best-selling author i guarantee you each and every one of them has something going for them that'll make somebody say hey you know that's interesting i might be interested in that and those are the kind of people you want to deal with we know i i'd like to shift gears for a second and talk about uh something uh different but you bring it up in the book or actually you've introduced on page 207 which is called the overwhelm syndrome and this relates to the huge quantity of action decisions that everyone makes in their lives each and every day and specifically how we deal with it which in which in some cases and we hear it all the time is people just feel overwhelmed um and you give some specific steps uh and recommendations to people when they feel overwhelmed uh could you talk a little bit about that i think i have some real interest to our membership yeah because everybody i think really has that problem and and i'll tell you something there's a there's a uh whatever the opposite or red flag is it's a it's a good flag it's a good sign you're doing something right if you're overwhelmed it's probably because you're taking a lot of action and one of the the things i think bill and dan i think you both agree with me is the more action you take the more that phone's gonna ring the more emails coming in and things are going to happen and and boy you got speech offers and you got things going on all over the place and frankly i'm in that position right now i i don't know where i i i don't know where to turn i just finished writing a 41 page ad 41 pages that um that has got to go out next the week after next and uh it it took quite a bit of my time i got an article that i got to get out and be ready to email it out to be emailed out every saturday and uh you know and then i've got a hundred medium projects and five thousand unimportant projects the kind that you just put aside and you're going to get to someday and every so many years i just throw out a bunch of uh papers everyone has this problem so i want to say first of all it's probably a good sign but if it's really getting to you as i say in the book the first step that you should take is just to mentally call time out and just come to a complete stop because you don't want to be to use a cliche a chicken running around like a chicken without a head you're not going to be any good if you're not calm and you don't have things under control and i always just take a deep breath and i just kind of stand back and i get a big picture of what's going on and i get back to priorities and i try to ask myself okay you know what what's what's the first thing that really needs to be done and what i found and and we i talked about drucker earlier what drucker said that was really uh good is uh it's not so important it's not as important to do the right uh it's more important to do the right thing than to do things right and i've got a um a sign on my wall that says uh that i made up probably 25 years ago uh and it says uh does it matter question mark if so how much does it matter and oh it took me years and years to get to this point but i found out the quickest way to get a lot of projects done is just not do them and since i'm obsessive compulsive that was really heresy to me i i didn't think i could ever do it but i i got a credit drucker with with that one i just found that a lot of things don't have to be done when you if you ask yourself if you say well i got to do this because it's such and such and or so and so will be mad you got to ask yourself so what harry brown pointed this out years ago and he was one of my early mentors not face-to-face mentor but through his his brilliant book how i found freedom in an unfree world and he said something that i'd never thought about before he said a huge every human being has unlimited desires but limited time and if you think about it it's absolutely true and anybody who's out there stirring the bushes and taking a lot of action you have an unlimited number of things to do you can never catch up and that's why i say you got to stand back and get a big picture perspective of the battlefield and say well say to yourself well if i don't do this project or that project what's the downside you know it's not a terminal illness you're going to die you're going to die or something like that and the third thing is after you've calmed down you just eliminate everything that isn't crucial to your chief objectives like i know i have to get an e-letter out every week i knew i had to get this ad out i know i have to get ready for the speech or that speech and there's a lot of other things you'd like to do there's a lot of things that might be interesting to do there's a lot of things that might make make you money but if they're not related to the most important things they're key objectives you've got to learn to let go and then as you know i in there i say step four is something i adopted about 25 30 years ago don't try to do everything just do something you've got to get started nothing is as cathartic for me as getting to work i tend to stew a lot and if i've got a very big office you know i've got i work in a 1,100 square foot office and i'm very stingy it's all for me i don't share and i walk and i walk and i pace a tremendous amount and if i it's okay if i'm thinking good thoughts but if i start stewing about all the problems and what am i going to do first i find that it gets worse and worse and worse but when i sit down and i just start taking action and i just do something or 18 projects that i have to do but if i just get working on one of them that's the important thing and so you just begin and you concentrate exclusively on that one project and then when it's done you just work calmly at a comfortable pace even if there's allocators at your door you just work away comfortably and then when that project is done you don't stop until you get it done when that project's done then you roll up your sleeves you take a deep breath and you say okay now i'm going to go on to the next item and you know what you won't ever get everything done and when they put you in the ground you are not going to have every piece of paper filed away and everything in the right place and every project done it's just called life but you do the best you can but above all do something do something get started you can't do everything but you can do one thing get started you know you and i we work so much alike i mean i first of all i i find a big thing in what you just said for a lot of entrepreneurs a real liberating thing is to get over the idea that they're supposed to get quote caught up no you don't you never get caught up and you never do and caught up means you're either dead or you're or you're not doing a very good job at whatever business or profession you're in and by the way i've had that conversation repeatedly with every wife um every girlfriend and every staff person and the question is always when are we going to get caught up and the answer is we're not supposed to be caught right i agree totally and and the work style thing i mean i occasionally things will get out of control for me and i'll just have i call it a shovel day where i'm just going to go in and shovel i'm not going to try and fire or i'm just going to pick something work on it until it's done pick take the next thing and just shovel away great feeling isn't yeah i just find it to be like a medicine i mean all of a sudden i start feeling better there's nothing like just rolling up your sleeves and just starting to calmly work on something and um after five minutes 10 minutes 15 doesn't look like much is happening and i'm always amazed at how little gets done in five minutes but how much gets done in three or four hours yeah and conversely the most sickening thing is as you said stewing or walking around looking at it all and worrying that you can never get it all done yeah and i have to fight that because i'm i'm someone because i'm i'm i'm i'm very compulsive and i'm very high energy and i i tend to make people nervous when i talk my wife always says that i've been probably been responsible for a lot of strokes but i got to be careful because if i take myself and i always i'm hard on myself i always say to myself ringer you know what you're really doing you're using as an excuse to stall just keep walking around just keep stewing but if you really want to and and i really hit this hard and in uh in my keynote address on action people and i and i think i touch on this in people there's been a great falsehood that's been that's been handed from generation to generation that is this whole issue of how do i get motivated to take action and the truth and this is heresy and conventional wisdom motivational circles and all these these these uh motivational speakers who say it they're doing a disservice to their audiences the truth is you don't wait to be uh you don't wait to become motivated to take action you have free will and free will means you have the power of choice and you can force yourself to take action and it's like magic when you take action motivation will follow
